title: States Weekly: June 17, 2026
text:
States Weekly: June 17, 2026

Newsletter
by

Aryan D’Rozario

Published June 17, 2026

Big News: Tamil Nadu issues Draft Notification of TNERC (Battery Energy Storage Systems) Regulations, 2026; Uttarakhand announces the Uttarakhand Science, Technology and Innovation Policy 2026; Maharashtra decentralizes approval processes for government land deals; Haryana issues a set of austerity and resource-management guidelines applicable to government departments; and more.Health Andhra Pradesh launches a pilot initiative to harness Artificial Intelligence (AI) in detecting diabetic retinopathy. Under the scheme, images of patients’ eyes will be analyzed by AI systems to determine the severity of retinopathy.Source: New Indian ExpressManipur launches 13 Mobile Medical Units (MMUs). The MMUs will provide a comprehensive range of medical services, health education, awareness programs, and referral services when required.Source: Imphal TimesIn detail: Press ReleaseClimate Change and EnergyDelhi temporarily relaxes Regulation 172 of the DERC (Terms and Conditions for Determination of Tariff) Regulations, 2017. The order, issued by the Delhi Electricity Regulatory Commission (DERC), allows DISCOMs to recover a higher Fuel and Power Purchase Adjustment Surcharge (FPPAS) from consumers.Source: Solar QuarterIn detail: OrderKarnataka releases Draft Connectivity and General Network Access to the Intra-State Transmission and State Distribution System Regulations, 2026. The regulations, released by the Karnataka Electricity Regulatory Commission (KERC) aim to create a more structured and transparent framework for open access and network connectivity across the state.Source: Solar QuarterIn detail: Draft KERC (Connectivity and General Network Accessto the Intra-State Transmission and State Distribution System) Regulations, 2026Tamil Nadu issues Draft Notification of TNERC (Battery Energy Storage Systems) Regulations, 2026. The regulations, issued by the Tamil Nadu Electricity Regulatory Commission (TNERC) provide a comprehensive framework for battery energy storage systems in the state.Source: MercomIn detail: Draft Notification of TNERC (Battery Energy Storage Systems) Regulations, 2026Meghalaya issues Draft Meghalaya State Electricity Regulatory Commission (State Grid Code) Regulations, 2026. The regulations, issued by the Meghalaya State Electricity Regulatory Commission (MSERC), set out guidelines for the planning, operation, scheduling, and protection of the state power grid.Source: MercomIn detail: Draft Meghalaya State Electricity Regulatory Commission (State Grid Code)Regulations, 2026Punjab approves an additional surcharge for consumers under the Punjab State Power Corporation (PSPCL). The order, issued by the Punjab State Electricity Regulatory Commission (PSERC), allows for an additional surcharge of INR $0.013 (INR 1.13)/kWh for full open access consumers.Source: MVA PulseWest Bengal mandates that all state government offices move to prepaid smart electricity meters. The new guidelines also require departments, autonomous bodies, and government-controlled entities to clear outstanding power dues either in a lump sum or over a period of 300 days.Source: Economic TimesFinanceNothing critical to report this week.Industrial Policy and Business Regulations  Uttarakhand announces the Uttarakhand Science, Technology and Innovation Policy 2026. The policy is designed to provide a common platform for youth, researchers, startups, scientists, and grassroots innovators and encourages the merging of traditional knowledge with modern technology.Source: The News MillLand Acquisition and Labor Regulations  Maharashtra decentralizes approval processes for government land deals. District Collectors and Divisional Commissioners can now approve the sale, transfer, redevelopment, and change of use of government land within certain land value restrictions.Source: Economic TimesIn detail: OrderInfrastructure and GovernanceBihar mandates approvals for all investment proposals to be issued within 30 days. If no decision is taken by the authorities concerned within a 30-day time limit, “deemed clearance” will automatically be granted.Source: PSU WatchIn detail: Press Release  (Hindi)Delhi introduces an incentive scheme for its employees who commute using the metro or other public transport services. Under the scheme, employees opting to use a common mobility card issued by the Delhi Transport Corporation (DTC) will be eligible for an additional incentive equivalent to 10 percent of their admissible transport allowance.Source: Times of IndiaHaryana allows families residing on village ‘shamlat’ land (common-use land) for over 20 years to apply for ownership rights. The applications for ownership transfers will be open until January 16, 2027.Source: Millennium PostIn detail: Press ReleaseMadhya Pradesh withdraws the two-child restriction linked to government service in the draft Madhya Pradesh Civil Services Rules. The rule prescribed a maximum limit of two children for eligibility for government employment.Source: New Indian ExpressTamil Nadu launches the ‘Singappen’ (Lioness) Special Force (SSF). The SSF will operate as a specialized police unit for women's safety.Source: The News MinuteIn detail: Press ReleaseUttar Pradesh launches Ganga (Government Assisted Network for Growth and Advancement). The scheme aims to provide high-speed fiber-based broadband connectivity to thousands of rural households across Uttar Pradesh.Source: Hindustan TimesAssam restricts issuance of new Aadhaar cards for individuals above 18 years of age. The rule does not apply to Scheduled Tribes, Schedules Castes, and Tea Garden communities until March 2027.Source: Asianet NewsIn detail: Cabinet DecisionKerala approves a free bus travel scheme for women and transgender individuals. The new rules will apply on “ordinary services” of the Kerala State Road Transport Corporation’s (KSRTC) buses from June 15, 2026 onwards.Source: The StatesmanIn detail: NotificationMeghalaya launches a One-Time Settlement (OTS) scheme aimed at providing relief to commercial vehicle owners by waiving outstanding taxes and penalties. Commercial vehicle owners will receive an 80 percent waiver on tax dues and penalties, and a 90 percent waiver on penalties related to their vehicle’s Fitness Certificates.Source: Times of IndiaIn detail: Press ReleaseChhattisgarh approves the transfer of yoga-related functions from the Social Welfare Department to the Medical Education Department. The decision will integrate yoga with healthcare and improve training, research, and academic coordination in the field of wellness and traditional medicinal systems.Source: Indian MastermindsIn detail: Cabinet DecisionOdisha announces that education in government schools and colleges will be provided free of cost from kindergarten to postgraduate level. The policy intends to make education universally accessible.Source: Telegraph IndiaOdisha publishes Operational Guidelines for Promotion of Heritage Properties. The framework introduces incentives and capital subsidies for property owners who restore and repurpose historical structures built before 1950.Source: Odisha BytesKarnataka announces free bus passes for students attending schools and colleges within the state.Source: ETV BharatIn detail: NotificationWater and SanitationNothing critical to report this week.Center – State Relations   Nothing critical to report this week.Any OtherHimachal Pradesh decides to grant full salary to employees pursuing higher education while on approved study leave. Previously, employees on approved study leave would only be granted a portion of their regular pay.Source: HR KathaIn detail: Press ReleaseBihar disallows government schoolteachers from teaching at coaching centers, private tuition classes and other commercial educational institutions.Source: India TV NewsHaryana issues a set of austerity and resource-management guidelines applicable to government departments. The measures, which come into immediate effect and will remain operational till September 2026 include restrictions on foreign travel, virtual meetings when possible, reduction in fuel consumption targets, energy-saving measures in government buildings, and more.Source: TribuneIn detail: Press Release
